political.s: FEC data shows Kroger's PAC raised ~$140K with an 83% lean toward Republican candidates (rep%: $14,500 to Democrats vs. $17,500+ to Republicans per provided data). OpenSecrets reports total 2024-cycle contributions of $856,339 across affiliates and $1.6M in federal lobbying spend in 2024, consistent with a right-leaning but business-pragmatic posture. The company focuses lobbying on food retail, labor, and regulatory issues.

charity.s: Kroger directs more than $300 million annually to communities through corporate giving and its two foundations — The Kroger Co. Foundation and the Zero Hunger | Zero Waste Foundation. Since 2017, the company has donated over 3.9 billion meals in food and funds, including 815 million pounds of surplus food rescued for donation. Key partners include Feeding America and No Kid Hungry, with customers directing an additional $46 million via Community Rewards.
charity.focus: Hunger relief, food security, disaster response, community development

environment.s: Kroger has set a 30% GHG reduction goal from its 2018 baseline, but its market-based Scope 1 and 2 emissions actually rose 3.8% year-over-year from 2022 to 2023. Green America and other watchdogs have criticized the company for lagging behind peers like ALDI, Whole Foods, and Target on phasing out climate-warming HFC refrigerants, and the company's 100% infrared leak-detector rollout target was delayed from 2024 to 2026. Kroger has achieved 82% waste diversion from landfill and reduced food waste by over 26% since 2017.

labor.s: Kroger has faced multiple NLRB findings, including a ruling that a subsidiary illegally stopped collecting union dues and a separate finding that a facility's parking lot policy unlawfully restricted off-duty union organizing activity. OSHA data shows annual labor hours per Kroger store in California decreased 13.5% from 2019 to 2023, with union members citing chronic understaffing and inadequate safety investment. The UFCW and other unions have filed unfair labor practice charges and authorized strikes, reflecting an adversarial labor-management relationship. Violation Tracker records over $3.18B in total penalties since 2000 across 316 records.

dei.s: Kroger maintains an active DEI department and has been recognized as a top-three 'Best Workplace for Diverse Professionals' by Mogul in 2024 and earned a perfect score on the Disability Equality Index for the fifth consecutive year. The company's Framework for Action DEI plan covers disability, ethnicity, LGBTQ, and veteran status, and targets growing diverse supplier spend to $10B by 2030. However, in its April 2025 10-K filing, Kroger deleted the standalone DEI section and folded ESG commentary under broader 'sustainability risks,' signaling a partial pullback on explicit DEI disclosures.

animals.s: Kroger has a formal Animal Welfare Policy rooted in the Five Domains framework and maintains goals for cage-free eggs and gestation-crate-free pork, and the ASPCA 2024 Supermarket Scorecard recognized Kroger as one of four chains making progress on farm animal welfare. However, Kroger continues to sell conventionally raised meat products and the ASPCA noted Kroger is still not reporting progress on pigs, trailing leaders Whole Foods and Sprouts. The company sells animal products and does not hold a cruelty-free certification.

guns.s: Kroger does not sell firearms across its standard grocery banner stores, and has formally exited the firearms and ammunition business entirely, including phasing out all gun sales at its Fred Meyer stores in 2019. The company also requests that customers not openly carry firearms in its stores and has called on lawmakers to strengthen background checks. This makes Kroger one of the few major retailers to have proactively and fully exited the gun sales business.

privacy.s: A Consumer Reports investigation found that Kroger's loyalty program collects extensive shopper data, builds detailed consumer profiles, and sells that data to more than 50 third-party companies including data brokers, tobacco companies, and financial entities, generating an estimated $527 million from its 'precision marketing' arm in 2024. The investigation also found inaccuracies in shopper profiles and raised concerns about surveillance pricing and discriminatory discount practices. Kroger denies personalizing product prices but admits to using demographic and behavioral data to tailor promotions.

execPay.s: Former CEO Rodney McMullen received $15.6 million in total compensation for fiscal year 2024, while the median Kroger worker earned $34,213 — yielding a CEO-to-median-worker pay ratio of 457:1, down slightly from 502:1 the prior year. McMullen resigned in March 2025 following a board ethics investigation into his personal conduct. The 457:1 ratio places Kroger well above industry and cross-sector averages, highlighting a significant pay disparity between top leadership and frontline associates.

primaryOffenses.category: off-label or unapproved promotion of medical products
